engineIntelligence.h2: Fiat Marea Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Fiat Marea in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Fiat Marea 817A engine - diesel 1.9L replacement UK
knownFailures: Cylinder head cracking - cracks develop between cylinders 3-4, causing coolant loss and terminal failure.
EGR valve clogging - carbon buildup restricts flow, causing rough idle and reduced power on high-mileage units.

cards.altText: Fiat Marea 182A1000 engine - petrol 2.0L replacement UK
knownFailures: Head gasket failure - weak MLS gasket fails prematurely, causing overheating and coolant/oil mixing.
Crankshaft pulley separation - rubber damping fails, risking cambelt damage and valve interference.

cards.altText: Fiat Marea 185A6000 engine - diesel 2.4L replacement UK
knownFailures: High pressure fuel pump failure - pump wear causes hard starting, power loss and fuel pressure codes.
Five-cylinder balance issues - rare units require specialist knowledge for correct replacement fitment.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £2,200-£3,500 (supply only) Estimated from comparable 2.4 JTD units - actual quotes may vary.

engineGuide.h2: Fiat Marea Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Fiat Marea across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 1.9L Diesel
entries.history: The foundational 1.9 JTD unit for Marea, delivering 105hp with common-rail injection-prone to head cracks between cylinders 3-4.
commonFailures: Cylinder head cracking between cylinders 3-4 (terminal failure, 120k+ miles)
EGR valve carbon buildup causing rough idle and power loss
Injector seal wear leading to diesel knock and hard starting
entries.cta: Get quotes for 817A Fiat Marea engine replacement

entries.title: 1.9L Diesel
entries.history: Late-production 1.9 JTD with 110hp output and revised ECU mapping for improved emissions and drivability.
commonFailures: Cylinder head cracking persists on high-mileage examples
Turbo actuator sticking causing limp mode
DPF-related issues on post-2005 units with added emissions hardware
entries.cta: Get quotes for 192A1000 Fiat Marea engine replacement

entries.title: 1.9L Diesel
entries.history: Alternative 1.9 JTD code for specific market variants, mechanically similar to 817A with minor calibration differences.
commonFailures: Head cracking between cylinders 3-4 (same vulnerability as 817A)
Crankshaft pulley rubber separation risking cambelt damage
entries.cta: Get quotes for 182A6000 Fiat Marea engine replacement

entries.title: 2.4L Diesel
entries.history: Five-cylinder 2.4 JTD delivering 130hp-rare in UK, sought by enthusiasts for smooth power delivery and torque.
commonFailures: High-pressure fuel pump wear causing hard starting and power loss
Five-cylinder balance shaft wear on high-mileage units
Specialist knowledge required for correct replacement fitment
entries.cta: Get quotes for 185A6000 Fiat Marea engine replacement

entries.title: 2.4L Diesel
entries.history: Late-production 2.4 JTD with revised calibration, same five-cylinder architecture with minor emissions updates.
commonFailures: HP pump failure remains the dominant failure point
Turbocharger wastegate sticking on high-mileage examples
entries.cta: Get quotes for 186A1000 Fiat Marea engine replacement

entries.title: 1.9L Diesel
entries.history: Indirect-injection 1.9 TD predecessor to JTD, simpler design with 75hp output-robust but less efficient.
commonFailures: Injector pump wear causing hard starting and smoke
Timing belt tensioner failure if service intervals missed
Generally robust but high-mileage units suffer oil consumption
entries.cta: Get quotes for 182A2000 Fiat Marea engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: The iconic 2.0 20V unit delivering 147hp, defining the Marea's sporting character-infamous for head gasket weakness.
commonFailures: Head gasket failure (weak MLS design, 80k-120k miles typical)
Crankshaft pulley rubber separation risking cambelt/valve damage
Oil consumption from valve stem seal wear on high-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for 182A1000 Fiat Marea engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: Revised 2.0 20V with minor updates for 1999-2002 models, same core architecture with improved ancillaries.
commonFailures: Head gasket failure persists despite minor revisions
Crank pulley separation remains a critical failure point
VIS system faults on later variants causing intake issues
entries.cta: Get quotes for B782B Fiat Marea engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: 2.0 20V VIS variant with Variable Intake System, delivering 155hp for enhanced mid-range torque and response.
commonFailures: VIS actuator failure causing loss of variable intake function
Head gasket and crank pulley issues shared with standard 20V
Intake manifold carbon buildup affecting VIS operation
entries.cta: Get quotes for 162A2000 Fiat Marea engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Petrol
entries.history: Entry-level 1.6 16V petrol delivering 103hp, reliable when maintained but timing belt critical for longevity.
commonFailures: Timing belt tensioner failure if service intervals missed
Valve stem seal wear causing oil consumption after 70k miles
Generally robust but interference engine-belt failure = bent valves

entries.title: 1.8L Petrol
entries.history: Mid-range 1.8 16V unit with 113hp, offering a balance of refinement and economy for everyday driving.
commonFailures: Timing belt tensioner wear requiring proactive replacement
Throttle body carbon buildup causing hesitation on city-driven examples
Oil consumption from piston ring wear on high-mileage units

entries.title: 1.6L Petrol/CNG
entries.history: Rare BiPower variant running on petrol or methane (CNG), offering lower fuel costs but limited UK availability.
commonFailures: CNG injector wear causing rough running on gas mode
Fuel system switching faults between petrol and CNG
Specialist knowledge required for diagnosis and replacement
entries.cta: Get quotes for B784A BiPower Fiat Marea engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Fiat Marea Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Fiat Marea's engine reliability is dominated by two key families: the 2.0 20V petrol (notorious for head gasket failure) and the 1.9 JTD diesel (prone to cylinder head cracking). Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the cost breakdowns below to decide whether repair or replacement offers the best value for your Marea.

problems.h4: Fiat Marea 2.0 20V Head Gasket Failure - Why These Engines Overheat Prematurely
problems.rootCause: The original multi-layer steel (MLS) head gasket is too thin for the 20V's thermal cycling, allowing coolant/oil crossover under sustained load.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder head, replace gasket with upgraded MLS unit, resurface head, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if block/head undamaged; risk of recurrence if root cause not addressed

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt engine with upgraded gasket, bench-tested, 12-month warranty included
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage cars; eliminates risk of hidden damage; warranty protection

problems.recommendation: On a Marea worth £500-£2,000, a full rebuilt engine at £1,300-£2,200 often makes more sense than a £800-£1,300 head gasket repair that may not address underlying wear. We recommend replacement if the car has exceeded 100k miles or shows signs of block distortion.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 2.0 20V engine prices for your Fiat Marea

problems.group: Fiat Marea 1.9 JTD Cylinder Head Crack
problems.h4: Fiat Marea 1.9 JTD Cylinder Head Crack - The Terminal Failure Point
problems.affectedModels: Fiat Marea 1.9 JTD 105/110, Weekend JTD, HLX JTD (1999-2007, codes 817A, 182A6000, 192A1000)
problems.rootCause: Thermal stress concentrates between cylinders 3-4 on the 1.9 JTD head, causing micro-cracks that propagate into coolant leaks and terminal failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove and replace cylinder head with crack-tested unit, new gaskets, timing components
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if block is undamaged; requires specialist crack-testing to avoid repeat failure

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt engine with crack-tested head, upgraded components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable solution for high-mileage JTDs; eliminates risk of hidden block damage

problems.recommendation: Because head cracks often indicate broader thermal stress, we recommend a full rebuilt engine for JTDs over 140k miles. The £1,400-£2,800 rebuilt option with crack-tested head offers better long-term value than a head-only repair on a car valued under £2,000.
problems.cta: Get quotes for crack-tested 1.9 JTD Fiat Marea engines

problems.h4: Fiat Marea Crankshaft Pulley Failure - The Silent Cambelt Killer
problems.rootCause: The rubber damping element in the crankshaft pulley deteriorates with age and heat, allowing the outer ring to separate and interfere with the cambelt.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace crankshaft pulley with upgraded unit, inspect cambelt for damage
repairOptions.longevity: Critical preventative fix; must be done before pulley separation occurs

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace damaged cambelt, tensioners, and bent valves if interference occurred
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if valve damage is limited; often uneconomical on low-value Marea

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with new pulley, cambelt kit, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best option if valve damage is confirmed; avoids costly partial repairs

problems.recommendation: If you hear rattling from the front of the engine or see wobble in the pulley, replace it immediately-£250-£450 preventative is far cheaper than a £1,300+ engine replacement after cambelt failure. For Marea owners, we advise inspecting the pulley at every major service.
problems.cta: Find upgraded crank pulley and rebuilt engine options for your Fiat Marea

problems.h4: Fiat Marea Timing Belt Failure - The Interference Engine Risk
problems.affectedModels: All Fiat Marea petrol and diesel variants (1996-2007)
problems.rootCause: Timing belt tensioner collapse or belt wear causes jump/snap; all Marea engines are interference design, so valve-piston contact is inevitable.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace belt, tensioner, idlers, water pump; reset timing marks
repairOptions.longevity: Essential preventative maintenance; must follow 60k mile/5 year interval

repairOptions.tier: Major - Valve Repair After Belt Failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove head, replace bent valves, guides, seals; reassemble and test
repairOptions.longevity: Only economical if block/pistons undamaged; rare on high-mileage Marea

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with new belt kit, 12-month warranty included
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable solution after belt failure; avoids hidden damage risks

problems.recommendation: Never gamble with timing belt intervals on a Marea. If the belt has snapped, a rebuilt engine at £1,300-£2,200 is usually better value than a £1,100-£2,100 valve repair that may uncover further damage. Prevention via £350-£600 belt kit replacement is always the smartest investment.
problems.cta: Compare timing belt service and rebuilt engine prices for your Fiat Marea

problems.h4: Fiat Marea 1.9 JTD Injector Failure - Diesel Knock and Hard Starting
problems.rootCause: High-pressure injector nozzles wear or seals fail, causing poor atomisation, diesel knock, hard starting and increased emissions.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove, test and recondition or replace injectors; recalibrate ECU
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if injectors are the only fault; requires specialist diagnostic equipment

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with Tested Injectors
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with bench-tested injectors, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best option if injectors failed due to broader fuel system wear or high mileage

problems.recommendation: If injector failure is isolated and the engine is otherwise healthy under 130k miles, reconditioning at £500-£900 can be cost-effective. For higher-mileage JTDs or multiple injector faults, a rebuilt engine with tested injectors offers better long-term reliability and warranty protection.
problems.cta: Get quotes for 1.9 JTD engines with tested injectors for your Fiat Marea

problems.group: Fiat Marea 2.4 JTD High-Pressure Pump Failure
problems.h4: Fiat Marea 2.4 JTD High-Pressure Pump Failure - Power Loss and Hard Starting
problems.rootCause: The high-pressure fuel pump's internal components wear, reducing rail pressure and causing hard starting, power loss and fault codes.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove and replace high-pressure pump; recalibrate fuel system
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if pump is the only fault; requires specialist calibration tools

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with Tested Pump
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt five-cylinder engine with bench-tested HP pump, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best option for high-mileage 2.4 JTDs; eliminates risk of secondary fuel system faults

problems.recommendation: Given the rarity and specialist nature of the 2.4 JTD, we recommend a full rebuilt engine with tested pump for units over 140k miles. The £2,200-£3,500 rebuilt option provides warranty protection and avoids the risk of cascading fuel system failures.
problems.cta: Enquire about 2.4 JTD rebuilt engine availability for your Fiat Marea

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Fiat Marea
finalCta.paragraph: The Fiat Marea's most common engine failures-head gasket on the 2.0 20V, head cracks on the 1.9 JTD, and crank pulley separation across the range-are well-documented and addressable. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Fiat Marea specialists who supply rebuilt engines with upgraded components designed to prevent repeat failures. Every unit includes a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery, so you can get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Fiat Marea Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Fiat Marea and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

items.title: Fiat Marea 1.9L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.9L diesel powered most UK Marea models from 1999 onwards, available in indirect-injection TD (75hp) and common-rail JTD (105-110hp) forms. The JTD units dominate replacement enquiries due to cylinder head cracking between cylinders 3-4.
items.cta: Get quotes for Fiat Marea 1.9L diesel engines

items.title: Fiat Marea 2.4L Diesel Engines
items.description: The five-cylinder 2.4L JTD was a rare, enthusiast-focused option delivering 130hp and smooth torque. Its complexity and low UK volume mean replacement units require specialist sourcing and careful code matching.
items.cta: Get quotes for Fiat Marea 2.4L diesel engines

items.title: Fiat Marea 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The entry-level 1.6L 16V petrol offered 103hp and reasonable economy, popular in ELX and SX trims. Timing belt maintenance is critical as all Marea petrol engines are interference design.
items.cta: Compare Fiat Marea 1.6L petrol engine prices

items.title: Fiat Marea 1.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.8L 16V provided a mid-range option with 113hp, balancing refinement and cost. Shares architecture with the 1.6L but with revised internals for extra displacement.
commonFailurePoints: piston ring wear on high mileage
items.cta: Compare Fiat Marea 1.8L petrol engine prices

items.title: Fiat Marea 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The iconic 2.0L 20V defined the Marea's sporting character with 147-155hp. Its head gasket weakness and crank pulley issues make it the most common petrol replacement enquiry.
items.cta: Compare Fiat Marea 2.0L petrol engine prices

engineYears.h2: Fiat Marea Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers all major engine changes across the Fiat Marea's UK production run (1996-2007), helping owners identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from specialists.

years.description: The Marea launched with indirect-injection diesels and 16V petrols, establishing the range before the pivotal JTD introduction.
keyChanges: UK launch with 1.6/1.8/2.0 petrol and 1.9 TD indirect diesel options
Weekend estate variant introduced alongside saloon
Early build quality issues prompt minor running changes
knownFor: Robust but unrefined 1.9 TD indirect diesel
2.0 20V establishes sporting reputation but early head gasket concerns emerge
Build quality and electrical gremlins common on early examples
years.cta: Get quotes for 1996-1999 Fiat Marea replacement engines
years.ticker: Fiat Marea 2.0 20V head gasket • Fiat Marea 1.9 TD injector pump • Fiat Marea timing belt replacement

years.description: The facelift introduced the game-changing 1.9 JTD common-rail diesel and revised 2.0 20V petrols, defining the Marea's peak popularity.
keyChanges: 1.9 JTD common-rail diesel (105hp) replaces indirect TD, transforming refinement and economy
Revised 2.0 20V petrols with minor updates (B782B code)
VIS (Variable Intake System) introduced on late 2.0 20V for 155hp output
knownFor: 1.9 JTD transforms Marea into a refined, economical cruiser but head cracking emerges as key failure
2.0 20V head gasket weakness becomes widespread knowledge among owners
Crankshaft pulley separation identified as critical preventative maintenance item
years.cta: Get quotes for 1999-2003 Fiat Marea replacement engines
years.ticker: Fiat Marea 1.9 JTD head crack • Fiat Marea 2.0 20V crank pulley • Fiat Marea VIS actuator fault

years.description: Final years saw Turkish production at TOFAŞ, with carry-over engines and increasing rarity of replacement units in the UK.
keyChanges: Production shifts to TOFAŞ plant in Turkey for cost reasons
Carry-over engine range with minor calibration updates for emissions
2.4 JTD five-cylinder remains rare enthusiast option
knownFor: Turkish-built units can have different trim/spec but engines remain compatible
Replacement engine availability becomes more challenging for rare codes like 2.4 JTD
High-mileage examples dominate the market; rebuilt engines increasingly preferred over used
years.cta: Enquire about late-production Fiat Marea engine availability

faq.h2: Fiat Marea Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Fiat Marea engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Typical rebuilt engine prices range from £900 for a 1.6 petrol to £3,500 for a rare 2.4 JTD, with the common 1.9 JTD and 2.0 20V falling between £1,300-£2,800 supply-only.
keyPoints: 1.6/1.8 petrol rebuilt: £900-£1,800 supply only
2.0 20V petrol rebuilt: £1,300-£2,200 with upgraded head gasket
1.9 JTD diesel rebuilt: £1,400-£2,800 with crack-tested head
Supply & fit adds £400-£800 labour depending on garage
items.cta: Compare live Fiat Marea engine prices from UK specialists

items.question: What is the most reliable Fiat Marea engine to buy?
items.answer: The 1.6 16V petrol (B784A) and pre-JTD 1.9 TD diesel (182A2000) are generally robust if maintained, but the 1.9 JTD's head cracking risk makes rebuilt units preferable to used.
keyPoints: 1.6 16V petrol: reliable with timely timing belt changes
1.9 TD indirect diesel: simple, robust but less efficient than JTD
Avoid used 1.9 JTD without proof of crack testing
Rebuilt 2.0 20V with upgraded gasket offers best performance reliability
items.cta: Find rebuilt Fiat Marea engines with warranty protection

items.question: Why do Fiat Marea 1.9 JTD engines crack between cylinders 3-4?
items.answer: Thermal stress concentrates in the cylinder head casting between cylinders 3-4 on the 1.9 JTD, causing micro-cracks that propagate into coolant leaks and terminal failure.
keyPoints: Failure typically occurs 120k-180k miles on high-use examples
Crack-tested replacement heads are essential to avoid repeat failure
Rebuilt engines with crack-tested heads offer best long-term value
Early symptoms: unexplained coolant loss, white exhaust smoke
items.cta: Get quotes for crack-tested 1.9 JTD Fiat Marea engines

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on a Fiat Marea?
items.answer: With Marea values typically £500-£2,000, engine replacement only makes sense if the body is rust-free and you plan to keep the car long-term; otherwise consider part-exchange.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engine cost often exceeds car's market value
Worthwhile if body/chassis is excellent and you love the car
Rebuilt engine with warranty adds reliability vs buying another used Marea
Get multiple quotes before deciding-prices vary significantly
items.cta: Compare Fiat Marea engine quotes to assess value vs replacement

items.question: Can I fit a Fiat Brava/Bravo engine in my Marea?
items.answer: While some engine codes overlap (e.g., B784A 1.6 16V), Marea-specific engine mounts, ancillaries and ECU mapping mean direct swaps are rarely straightforward-always verify compatibility first.
keyPoints: Same engine code doesn't guarantee plug-and-play fitment
Marea Weekend estate has different mounting points vs saloon
ECU and wiring loom differences can cause running issues
Use registration lookup to confirm exact compatible codes
items.cta: Verify engine compatibility for your specific Fiat Marea variant

items.question: What causes Fiat Marea 2.0 20V head gasket failure?
items.answer: The original multi-layer steel (MLS) head gasket is too thin for the 20V's thermal cycling, allowing coolant/oil crossover under sustained load or overheating events.
keyPoints: Typical failure mileage: 80k-120k miles
Upgraded MLS gaskets in rebuilt units address the root cause
Crankshaft pulley failure can cause overheating that triggers gasket failure
Symptoms: coolant loss, oil contamination, overheating warning
items.cta: Compare rebuilt 2.0 20V engines with upgraded gaskets

items.question: How long should a Fiat Marea engine last with proper maintenance?
items.answer: Well-maintained 1.6/1.8 petrol and pre-JTD 1.9 TD units can reach 150k-200k miles; 2.0 20V and 1.9 JTD typically require major work or replacement by 120k-150k miles.
keyPoints: Timing belt changes every 60k miles/5 years critical for all variants
Regular oil changes with correct spec extend engine life significantly
Address coolant leaks and overheating immediately to prevent cascade failures
Rebuilt engines with upgraded components can match or exceed original lifespan
items.cta: Find Fiat Marea engines with 12-month warranty for peace of mind

items.question: Where can I buy a replacement Fiat Marea engine in the UK?
items.answer: Specialist engine finders, Fiat breakers and EU importers supply Marea engines; EngineMarket compares quotes from vetted UK specialists to ensure code compatibility and warranty coverage.
keyPoints: Always confirm engine code matches your vehicle exactly
Rebuilt units from specialists include warranty vs used from breakers
Rare engines (2.4 JTD, BiPower) may require EU sourcing
Registration lookup ensures you get the correct compatible unit
items.cta: Get free Fiat Marea engine quotes from trusted UK suppliers

items.question: What is the difference between reconditioned, rebuilt and used Fiat Marea engines?
items.answer: Used engines are low-mileage donor units with unknown history; reconditioned units have wear items replaced; rebuilt engines undergo full disassembly with upgraded components addressing known failures.
keyPoints: Used: cheapest but highest risk; typically 3-6 month warranty
Reconditioned: balanced cost/reliability; 12-month warranty typical
Rebuilt: highest reliability with upgraded internals; best for high-mileage cars
Always confirm warranty terms and what work was performed
items.cta: Compare Fiat Marea engine types and prices side-by-side

items.question: Does a Fiat Marea engine replacement include supply and fit?
items.answer: Engine prices quoted are typically supply-only; supply & fit services add £400-£800 labour and include removal, fitting, fluids and post-installation testing by a specialist.
keyPoints: Supply-only allows you to choose your own fitting garage
Supply & fit saves time and ensures specialist installation
Confirm what's included: ancillaries, belt kit, ECU coding if needed
Nationwide delivery available for supply-only orders
items.cta: Find Fiat Marea engine supply & fit deals near you
